risk_if_not_implemented: Without Detonation Chambers (Sandboxes), residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_1.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Detonation Chambers (Sandboxes) (IRO-15) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Malicious Code Protection (Anti-Malware)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize antimalware technologies to detect and eradicate malicious code.
compensating_control_2.justification: Malicious Code Protection (Anti-Malware) (END-04) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Detonation Chambers (Sandboxes) (IRO-15) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
